notice: v0.40 displays uncapped raw net pressure scores, classifies assets into primary pressure buckets, uses movement/conflict as tags, writes score snapshots, and preserves live-only scoring discipline.

assets.name: U.S. Dollar Fundamental Pressure
assets.mainConflict: BLS CPI energy pressure (bls, +1.80) vs St. Louis financial stress (fed, -1.05)
assets.quick: Live-only scoring shows positive raw pressure from 27 counted rows. Top driver: BLS CPI energy pressure (bls, +1.80). Raw score is uncapped, source-weighted, and excludes non-live rows.

factors.name: Policy rate
factors.derived: Current central bank target rate or policy band
factors.effect: Primary effect: Higher relative policy rates usually support USD; lower or dovish policy usually pressures it.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Rate differentials
factors.derived: Relevant yield spread between countries or curves, usually 2Y and 10Y comparisons
factors.effect: Primary effect: Wider yield spreads in favor of USD usually support the currency pair; narrowing spreads usually pressure it.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Real yield differentials
factors.derived: Inflation-adjusted yield spread, often 10Y real yield vs peer real yield
factors.effect: Primary effect: Wider inflation-adjusted yield advantage usually supports USD; a weaker real-yield spread usually pressures it.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: 2Y yield
factors.derived: Official Treasury 2 Yr par yield: 4.18% on 2026-07-17
factors.effect: Higher 2Y yields usually mean stronger front-end policy pressure; lower 2Y yields reduce that pressure. The effect is direct for rate assets and indirect for other assets through policy, dollar, and risk channels.
factors.source: U.S. Treasury official Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: 10Y yield
factors.derived: Official Treasury 10 Yr par yield: 4.55% on 2026-07-17
factors.effect: Higher 10Y yields usually increase long-rate pressure and can pressure risk assets and gold; lower 10Y yields usually ease that pressure.
factors.source: U.S. Treasury official Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: Yield curve
factors.derived: Official Treasury curve spreads on 2026-07-17: 10Y-2Y 0.370 percentage points; 10Y-3M 0.700 percentage points.
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on USD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through rates, discount-rate pressure, or funding conditions.
factors.source: U.S. Treasury official Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates

factors.name: Real yields
factors.derived: 10Y TIPS real yield trend and level
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on USD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through rates, discount-rate pressure, or funding conditions.

factors.name: Breakeven inflation
factors.derived: 5Y and 10Y breakeven inflation
factors.effect: Context only: Breakevens affect USD through inflation expectations and real-yield pressure. Use it to confirm or challenge the read, not as the main driver.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: PCE pressure
factors.derived: PCE and Core PCE inflation readings
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Core PCE is important for policy expectations; hotter PCE usually supports rate/yield pressure and can support the dollar side of USD.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: GDP / growth trend
factors.derived: GDP growth, nowcast, or growth surprise vs prior/forecast
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Stronger relative growth can support USD by attracting capital and lifting rate expectations.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Retail sales / consumer demand
factors.derived: Retail sales headline/control group and surprise
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Stronger consumer data can support USD if it lifts growth and rate expectations.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Credit spreads
factors.derived: High-yield and investment-grade spreads vs history
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Credit spreads affect USD through risk appetite and funding-stress conditions.
factors.source: FRED / Federal Reserve public-source candidate with series rights check
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Financial conditions
factors.derived: Financial conditions index or composite of rates, dollar, equities, and credit
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Tighter conditions can support safe-haven currency demand and pressure high-beta FX linked to USD.
factors.source: FRED / Federal Reserve public-source candidate with series rights check
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Open interest
factors.derived: Futures open interest trend and change
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Open interest helps show conviction behind futures positioning for USD. Rising OI with a supportive COT read adds weight; falling OI weakens confirmation.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Crude inventories
factors.derived: Weekly U.S. commercial crude oil stock change
factors.effect: Low direct effect on USD. Watch it only if it spills into energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: Cushing inventories
factors.derived: Cushing crude stock level and weekly change
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on USD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: Product inventories
factors.derived: Gasoline and distillate stock changes
factors.effect: Low direct effect on USD. Watch it only if it spills into energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: Refinery utilization
factors.derived: Percent of refinery capacity utilized
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on USD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: U.S. production
factors.derived: U.S. crude or natural gas production estimate
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on USD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: Imports / exports
factors.derived: Petroleum or commodity trade flow changes
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on USD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: OPEC policy
factors.derived: OPEC official production targets, cuts, increases, compliance notes, and public monthly releases
factors.effect: Low direct effect on USD. Watch it only if it spills into rates, discount-rate pressure, or funding conditions.

factors.name: Weather HDD/CDD
factors.derived: Heating-degree days, cooling-degree days, and public weather/forecast deviations where available
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on USD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: LNG exports / power burn
factors.derived: LNG export flows and gas used for electricity generation
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on USD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: WASDE ending stocks
factors.derived: USDA WASDE ending stocks for wheat, corn, soybeans, cotton
factors.effect: Low direct effect on USD. Watch it only if it spills into food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Stock/use ratio
factors.derived: Ending stocks divided by total use
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on USD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Crop progress / condition
factors.derived: Planting, emergence, harvest progress, and good/excellent crop ratings
factors.effect: Low direct effect on USD. Watch it only if it spills into food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Export sales
factors.derived: Weekly export sales and shipments
factors.effect: Low direct effect on USD. Watch it only if it spills into food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Weather / drought
factors.derived: NOAA/NWS weather, drought monitor, rainfall, heat/freeze risk, and crop-region conditions where available
factors.effect: Low direct effect on USD. Watch it only if it spills into food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Global supply competitors
factors.derived: Brazil/Argentina/Black Sea/EU/Australia supply where relevant
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on USD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Ethanol / biofuel linkage
factors.derived: EIA/USDA ethanol production, corn use for ethanol, and biofuel-demand data
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on USD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: COT spec direction
factors.derived: CFTC COT net speculative positioning for the mapped futures contract; live values appear after Refresh CFTC COT Lane runs.
factors.effect: Spec direction shows trend participation. Net long speculative exposure can support the asset; net short exposure can pressure it, with inversion applied for Treasury yield assets and USD-base FX pairs.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports

factors.name: COT spec weekly change
factors.derived: Weekly change in speculative net exposure from the CFTC COT report.
factors.effect: Weekly change shows whether specs are adding to or reducing the directional positioning read. Adding exposure in the supportive direction strengthens the signal; reducing exposure weakens it.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports

factors.name: COT crowding risk
factors.derived: Recent percentile/range position of speculative net exposure where available.
factors.effect: Crowding risk separates participation from fragility. Stretched long positioning can create unwind risk; stretched short positioning can create short-covering risk.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports

factors.name: COT commercial / hedger extreme
factors.derived: Producer/merchant/processor/user positioning for commodity futures when available.
factors.effect: Commercial/hedger positioning is not an automatic signal. Extreme commercial long exposure can suggest value/support context; extreme commercial short exposure can warn that speculative long exposure may be crowded.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports

factors.name: COT spec-commercial conflict
factors.derived: Comparison of speculative positioning against commercial/hedger positioning where available.
factors.effect: Conflict means specs and commercials are meaningfully opposed. The trend participation read can still work, but reversal or unwind risk deserves more attention.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports

factors.name: EIA petroleum inventory balance
factors.derived: U.S. commercial crude oil stocks excluding SPR (W_EPC0_SAX_NUS_MBBL) latest 499,740 thousand barrels on 2016-09-30; previous 502,716 thousand barrels on 2016-09-23. Weekly change: -2,976 thousand barrels. | U.S. motor gasoline stocks (W_EPM0_SAX_NUS_MBBL) latest missing thousand barrels on None; previous missing thousand barrels on None. | U.S. distillate fuel oil stocks (W_EPD0_SAX_NUS_MBBL) latest missing thousand barrels on None; previous missing thousand barrels on None.
factors.effect: Contextual effect: EIA energy tightness can affect U.S. Dollar Fundamental Pressure through inflation pressure, rate expectations, and risk appetite. It is not the main direct driver.
factors.source: U.S. Energy Information Administration open data/API

scoreChangeLog.summary: USD is broadly stable versus the previous saved snapshot.

assets.name: Euro / U.S. Dollar
assets.mainConflict: St. Louis financial stress (fed, +0.97) vs BLS CPI energy pressure (bls, -1.60)
assets.quick: Live-only scoring shows negative raw pressure from 25 counted rows. Top driver: BLS CPI energy pressure (bls, -1.60). Raw score is uncapped, source-weighted, and excludes non-live rows.

factors.name: Policy rate
factors.derived: Current central bank target rate or policy band
factors.effect: Primary effect: Higher relative policy rates usually support EURUSD; lower or dovish policy usually pressures it.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Rate differentials
factors.derived: Relevant yield spread between countries or curves, usually 2Y and 10Y comparisons
factors.effect: Primary effect: Wider yield spreads in favor of EURUSD usually support the currency pair; narrowing spreads usually pressure it.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Real yield differentials
factors.derived: Inflation-adjusted yield spread, often 10Y real yield vs peer real yield
factors.effect: Primary effect: Wider inflation-adjusted yield advantage usually supports EURUSD; a weaker real-yield spread usually pressures it.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: 2Y yield
factors.derived: Official Treasury 2 Yr par yield: 4.18% on 2026-07-17
factors.effect: Higher 2Y yields usually mean stronger front-end policy pressure; lower 2Y yields reduce that pressure. The effect is direct for rate assets and indirect for other assets through policy, dollar, and risk channels.
factors.source: U.S. Treasury official Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: 10Y yield
factors.derived: Official Treasury 10 Yr par yield: 4.55% on 2026-07-17
factors.effect: Higher 10Y yields usually increase long-rate pressure and can pressure risk assets and gold; lower 10Y yields usually ease that pressure.
factors.source: U.S. Treasury official Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: Yield curve
factors.derived: Official Treasury curve spreads on 2026-07-17: 10Y-2Y 0.370 percentage points; 10Y-3M 0.700 percentage points.
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on EURUSD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through rates, discount-rate pressure, or funding conditions.
factors.source: U.S. Treasury official Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates

factors.name: Real yields
factors.derived: 10Y TIPS real yield trend and level
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on EURUSD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through rates, discount-rate pressure, or funding conditions.

factors.name: Breakeven inflation
factors.derived: 5Y and 10Y breakeven inflation
factors.effect: Context only: Breakevens affect EURUSD through inflation expectations and real-yield pressure. Use it to confirm or challenge the read, not as the main driver.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: PCE pressure
factors.derived: PCE and Core PCE inflation readings
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Core PCE is important for policy expectations; hotter PCE usually supports rate/yield pressure and can support the dollar side of EURUSD.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: GDP / growth trend
factors.derived: GDP growth, nowcast, or growth surprise vs prior/forecast
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Stronger relative growth can support EURUSD by attracting capital and lifting rate expectations.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Retail sales / consumer demand
factors.derived: Retail sales headline/control group and surprise
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Stronger consumer data can support EURUSD if it lifts growth and rate expectations.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Credit spreads
factors.derived: High-yield and investment-grade spreads vs history
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Credit spreads affect EURUSD through risk appetite and funding-stress conditions.
factors.source: FRED / Federal Reserve public-source candidate with series rights check
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Financial conditions
factors.derived: Financial conditions index or composite of rates, dollar, equities, and credit
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Tighter conditions can support safe-haven currency demand and pressure high-beta FX linked to EURUSD.
factors.source: FRED / Federal Reserve public-source candidate with series rights check
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Open interest
factors.derived: Futures open interest trend and change
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Open interest helps show conviction behind futures positioning for EURUSD. Rising OI with a supportive COT read adds weight; falling OI weakens confirmation.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: COT spec direction
factors.derived: Traders in Financial Futures - Futures Only for EURO FX - CHICAGO MERCANTILE EXCHANGE on 2026-07-14: Leveraged Funds long 89,976, short 143,667, net -53,691 (-6.72% of open interest), effective for this asset -6.72%.
factors.effect: Leveraged Funds are net short in the related currency futures contract, which is pressuring for this FX read. Effective positioning equals -6.72% of open interest. This reflects speculative participation, not a timing signal.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: COT spec weekly change
factors.derived: Weekly net change -8,230; effective weekly change -1.03% of open interest.
factors.effect: Weekly change shows whether speculative participation is getting stronger or weaker for Euro / U.S. Dollar. Adding exposure in the supportive direction strengthens the positioning read; reducing exposure weakens it.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: COT crowding risk
factors.derived: Spec effective positioning percentile over recent lookback: 1.90%.
factors.effect: Spec positioning is pressuring the asset but is stretched short, so short-covering can become supportive if other evidence turns.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

assets.name: British Pound / U.S. Dollar
assets.mainConflict: St. Louis financial stress (fed, +0.97) vs BLS CPI energy pressure (bls, -1.60)
assets.quick: Live-only scoring shows negative raw pressure from 25 counted rows. Top driver: BLS CPI energy pressure (bls, -1.60). Raw score is uncapped, source-weighted, and excludes non-live rows.

factors.name: Policy rate
factors.derived: Current central bank target rate or policy band
factors.effect: Primary effect: Higher relative policy rates usually support GBPUSD; lower or dovish policy usually pressures it.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Rate differentials
factors.derived: Relevant yield spread between countries or curves, usually 2Y and 10Y comparisons
factors.effect: Primary effect: Wider yield spreads in favor of GBPUSD usually support the currency pair; narrowing spreads usually pressure it.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Real yield differentials
factors.derived: Inflation-adjusted yield spread, often 10Y real yield vs peer real yield
factors.effect: Primary effect: Wider inflation-adjusted yield advantage usually supports GBPUSD; a weaker real-yield spread usually pressures it.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: 2Y yield
factors.derived: Official Treasury 2 Yr par yield: 4.18% on 2026-07-17
factors.effect: Higher 2Y yields usually mean stronger front-end policy pressure; lower 2Y yields reduce that pressure. The effect is direct for rate assets and indirect for other assets through policy, dollar, and risk channels.
factors.source: U.S. Treasury official Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: 10Y yield
factors.derived: Official Treasury 10 Yr par yield: 4.55% on 2026-07-17
factors.effect: Higher 10Y yields usually increase long-rate pressure and can pressure risk assets and gold; lower 10Y yields usually ease that pressure.
factors.source: U.S. Treasury official Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: Yield curve
factors.derived: Official Treasury curve spreads on 2026-07-17: 10Y-2Y 0.370 percentage points; 10Y-3M 0.700 percentage points.
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on GBPUSD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through rates, discount-rate pressure, or funding conditions.
factors.source: U.S. Treasury official Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates

factors.name: Real yields
factors.derived: 10Y TIPS real yield trend and level
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on GBPUSD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through rates, discount-rate pressure, or funding conditions.

factors.name: Breakeven inflation
factors.derived: 5Y and 10Y breakeven inflation
factors.effect: Context only: Breakevens affect GBPUSD through inflation expectations and real-yield pressure. Use it to confirm or challenge the read, not as the main driver.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: PCE pressure
factors.derived: PCE and Core PCE inflation readings
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Core PCE is important for policy expectations; hotter PCE usually supports rate/yield pressure and can support the dollar side of GBPUSD.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: GDP / growth trend
factors.derived: GDP growth, nowcast, or growth surprise vs prior/forecast
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Stronger relative growth can support GBPUSD by attracting capital and lifting rate expectations.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Retail sales / consumer demand
factors.derived: Retail sales headline/control group and surprise
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Stronger consumer data can support GBPUSD if it lifts growth and rate expectations.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Credit spreads
factors.derived: High-yield and investment-grade spreads vs history
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Credit spreads affect GBPUSD through risk appetite and funding-stress conditions.
factors.source: FRED / Federal Reserve public-source candidate with series rights check
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Financial conditions
factors.derived: Financial conditions index or composite of rates, dollar, equities, and credit
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Tighter conditions can support safe-haven currency demand and pressure high-beta FX linked to GBPUSD.
factors.source: FRED / Federal Reserve public-source candidate with series rights check
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Open interest
factors.derived: Futures open interest trend and change
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Open interest helps show conviction behind futures positioning for GBPUSD. Rising OI with a supportive COT read adds weight; falling OI weakens confirmation.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: COT spec direction
factors.derived: Traders in Financial Futures - Futures Only for BRITISH POUND - CHICAGO MERCANTILE EXCHANGE on 2026-07-14: Leveraged Funds long 62,437, short 33,896, net 28,541 (10.76% of open interest), effective for this asset 10.76%.
factors.effect: Leveraged Funds are net long in the related currency futures contract, which is supportive for this FX read. Effective positioning equals 10.76% of open interest. This reflects speculative participation, not a timing signal.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: COT spec weekly change
factors.derived: Weekly net change 10,562; effective weekly change 3.98% of open interest.
factors.effect: Weekly change shows whether speculative participation is getting stronger or weaker for British Pound / U.S. Dollar. Adding exposure in the supportive direction strengthens the positioning read; reducing exposure weakens it.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: COT crowding risk
factors.derived: Spec effective positioning percentile over recent lookback: 49.10%.
factors.effect: Spec positioning is not at a recent extreme, so crowding risk is not a major signal yet.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

assets.name: U.S. Dollar / Japanese Yen
assets.mainConflict: BLS CPI energy pressure (bls, +1.60) vs St. Louis financial stress (fed, -0.97)
assets.quick: Live-only scoring shows positive raw pressure from 25 counted rows. Top driver: BLS CPI energy pressure (bls, +1.60). Raw score is uncapped, source-weighted, and excludes non-live rows.

factors.name: Policy rate
factors.derived: Current central bank target rate or policy band
factors.effect: Primary effect: Higher relative policy rates usually support USDJPY; lower or dovish policy usually pressures it.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Rate differentials
factors.derived: Relevant yield spread between countries or curves, usually 2Y and 10Y comparisons
factors.effect: Primary effect: Wider yield spreads in favor of USDJPY usually support the currency pair; narrowing spreads usually pressure it.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Real yield differentials
factors.derived: Inflation-adjusted yield spread, often 10Y real yield vs peer real yield
factors.effect: Primary effect: Wider inflation-adjusted yield advantage usually supports USDJPY; a weaker real-yield spread usually pressures it.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: 2Y yield
factors.derived: Official Treasury 2 Yr par yield: 4.18% on 2026-07-17
factors.effect: Higher 2Y yields usually mean stronger front-end policy pressure; lower 2Y yields reduce that pressure. The effect is direct for rate assets and indirect for other assets through policy, dollar, and risk channels.
factors.source: U.S. Treasury official Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: 10Y yield
factors.derived: Official Treasury 10 Yr par yield: 4.55% on 2026-07-17
factors.effect: Higher 10Y yields usually increase long-rate pressure and can pressure risk assets and gold; lower 10Y yields usually ease that pressure.
factors.source: U.S. Treasury official Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: Yield curve
factors.derived: Official Treasury curve spreads on 2026-07-17: 10Y-2Y 0.370 percentage points; 10Y-3M 0.700 percentage points.
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on USDJPY. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through rates, discount-rate pressure, or funding conditions.
factors.source: U.S. Treasury official Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates

factors.name: Real yields
factors.derived: 10Y TIPS real yield trend and level
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on USDJPY. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through rates, discount-rate pressure, or funding conditions.

factors.name: Breakeven inflation
factors.derived: 5Y and 10Y breakeven inflation
factors.effect: Context only: Breakevens affect USDJPY through inflation expectations and real-yield pressure. Use it to confirm or challenge the read, not as the main driver.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: PCE pressure
factors.derived: PCE and Core PCE inflation readings
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Core PCE is important for policy expectations; hotter PCE usually supports rate/yield pressure and can support the dollar side of USDJPY.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: GDP / growth trend
factors.derived: GDP growth, nowcast, or growth surprise vs prior/forecast
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Stronger relative growth can support USDJPY by attracting capital and lifting rate expectations.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Retail sales / consumer demand
factors.derived: Retail sales headline/control group and surprise
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Stronger consumer data can support USDJPY if it lifts growth and rate expectations.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Credit spreads
factors.derived: High-yield and investment-grade spreads vs history
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Credit spreads affect USDJPY through risk appetite and funding-stress conditions.
factors.source: FRED / Federal Reserve public-source candidate with series rights check
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Financial conditions
factors.derived: Financial conditions index or composite of rates, dollar, equities, and credit
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Tighter conditions can support safe-haven currency demand and pressure high-beta FX linked to USDJPY.
factors.source: FRED / Federal Reserve public-source candidate with series rights check
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Open interest
factors.derived: Futures open interest trend and change
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Open interest helps show conviction behind futures positioning for USDJPY. Rising OI with a supportive COT read adds weight; falling OI weakens confirmation.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: COT spec direction
factors.derived: Traders in Financial Futures - Futures Only for JAPANESE YEN - CHICAGO MERCANTILE EXCHANGE on 2026-07-14: Leveraged Funds long 80,938, short 171,399, net -90,461 (-22.81% of open interest), effective for this asset 22.81%.
factors.effect: Leveraged Funds are net short in the quote-currency futures contract; after USD-base inversion this is supportive for USDJPY. Effective positioning equals 22.81% of open interest. Stronger quote-currency positioning usually pressures a USD-base pair.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: COT spec weekly change
factors.derived: Weekly net change -378; effective weekly change 0.10% of open interest.
factors.effect: Weekly change shows whether speculative participation is getting stronger or weaker for U.S. Dollar / Japanese Yen. Adding exposure in the supportive direction strengthens the positioning read; reducing exposure weakens it.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: COT crowding risk
factors.derived: Spec effective positioning percentile over recent lookback: 88.70%.
factors.effect: Spec positioning is not at a recent extreme, so crowding risk is not a major signal yet.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

scoreChangeLog.summary: USDJPY is broadly stable versus the previous saved snapshot.

assets.name: U.S. Dollar / Swiss Franc
assets.mainConflict: BLS CPI energy pressure (bls, +1.60) vs St. Louis financial stress (fed, -0.97)
assets.quick: Live-only scoring shows positive raw pressure from 25 counted rows. Top driver: BLS CPI energy pressure (bls, +1.60). Raw score is uncapped, source-weighted, and excludes non-live rows.

factors.name: Policy rate
factors.derived: Current central bank target rate or policy band
factors.effect: Primary effect: Higher relative policy rates usually support USDCHF; lower or dovish policy usually pressures it.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Rate differentials
factors.derived: Relevant yield spread between countries or curves, usually 2Y and 10Y comparisons
factors.effect: Primary effect: Wider yield spreads in favor of USDCHF usually support the currency pair; narrowing spreads usually pressure it.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Real yield differentials
factors.derived: Inflation-adjusted yield spread, often 10Y real yield vs peer real yield
factors.effect: Primary effect: Wider inflation-adjusted yield advantage usually supports USDCHF; a weaker real-yield spread usually pressures it.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: 2Y yield
factors.derived: Official Treasury 2 Yr par yield: 4.18% on 2026-07-17
factors.effect: Higher 2Y yields usually mean stronger front-end policy pressure; lower 2Y yields reduce that pressure. The effect is direct for rate assets and indirect for other assets through policy, dollar, and risk channels.
factors.source: U.S. Treasury official Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: 10Y yield
factors.derived: Official Treasury 10 Yr par yield: 4.55% on 2026-07-17
factors.effect: Higher 10Y yields usually increase long-rate pressure and can pressure risk assets and gold; lower 10Y yields usually ease that pressure.
factors.source: U.S. Treasury official Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: Yield curve
factors.derived: Official Treasury curve spreads on 2026-07-17: 10Y-2Y 0.370 percentage points; 10Y-3M 0.700 percentage points.
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on USDCHF. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through rates, discount-rate pressure, or funding conditions.
factors.source: U.S. Treasury official Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates

factors.name: Real yields
factors.derived: 10Y TIPS real yield trend and level
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on USDCHF. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through rates, discount-rate pressure, or funding conditions.

factors.name: Breakeven inflation
factors.derived: 5Y and 10Y breakeven inflation
factors.effect: Context only: Breakevens affect USDCHF through inflation expectations and real-yield pressure. Use it to confirm or challenge the read, not as the main driver.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: PCE pressure
factors.derived: PCE and Core PCE inflation readings
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Core PCE is important for policy expectations; hotter PCE usually supports rate/yield pressure and can support the dollar side of USDCHF.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: GDP / growth trend
factors.derived: GDP growth, nowcast, or growth surprise vs prior/forecast
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Stronger relative growth can support USDCHF by attracting capital and lifting rate expectations.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Retail sales / consumer demand
factors.derived: Retail sales headline/control group and surprise
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Stronger consumer data can support USDCHF if it lifts growth and rate expectations.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Credit spreads
factors.derived: High-yield and investment-grade spreads vs history
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Credit spreads affect USDCHF through risk appetite and funding-stress conditions.
factors.source: FRED / Federal Reserve public-source candidate with series rights check
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Financial conditions
factors.derived: Financial conditions index or composite of rates, dollar, equities, and credit
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Tighter conditions can support safe-haven currency demand and pressure high-beta FX linked to USDCHF.
factors.source: FRED / Federal Reserve public-source candidate with series rights check
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Open interest
factors.derived: Futures open interest trend and change
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Open interest helps show conviction behind futures positioning for USDCHF. Rising OI with a supportive COT read adds weight; falling OI weakens confirmation.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: COT spec direction
factors.derived: Traders in Financial Futures - Futures Only for SWISS FRANC - CHICAGO MERCANTILE EXCHANGE on 2026-07-14: Leveraged Funds long 9,951, short 19,451, net -9,500 (-8.76% of open interest), effective for this asset 8.76%.
factors.effect: Leveraged Funds are net short in the quote-currency futures contract; after USD-base inversion this is supportive for USDCHF. Effective positioning equals 8.76% of open interest. Stronger quote-currency positioning usually pressures a USD-base pair.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: COT spec weekly change
factors.derived: Weekly net change -2,282; effective weekly change 2.10% of open interest.
factors.effect: Weekly change shows whether speculative participation is getting stronger or weaker for U.S. Dollar / Swiss Franc. Adding exposure in the supportive direction strengthens the positioning read; reducing exposure weakens it.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: COT crowding risk
factors.derived: Spec effective positioning percentile over recent lookback: 92.50%.
factors.effect: Spec positioning is supportive but stretched near the high end of its recent range, so crowded-long unwind risk rises.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

scoreChangeLog.summary: USDCHF is broadly stable versus the previous saved snapshot.

assets.name: U.S. Dollar / Canadian Dollar
assets.mainConflict: BLS CPI energy pressure (bls, +1.60) vs St. Louis financial stress (fed, -0.97)
assets.quick: Live-only scoring shows positive raw pressure from 25 counted rows. Top driver: BLS CPI energy pressure (bls, +1.60). Raw score is uncapped, source-weighted, and excludes non-live rows.

factors.name: Policy rate
factors.derived: Current central bank target rate or policy band
factors.effect: Primary effect: Higher relative policy rates usually support USDCAD; lower or dovish policy usually pressures it.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Rate differentials
factors.derived: Relevant yield spread between countries or curves, usually 2Y and 10Y comparisons
factors.effect: Primary effect: Wider yield spreads in favor of USDCAD usually support the currency pair; narrowing spreads usually pressure it.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Real yield differentials
factors.derived: Inflation-adjusted yield spread, often 10Y real yield vs peer real yield
factors.effect: Primary effect: Wider inflation-adjusted yield advantage usually supports USDCAD; a weaker real-yield spread usually pressures it.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: 2Y yield
factors.derived: Official Treasury 2 Yr par yield: 4.18% on 2026-07-17
factors.effect: Higher 2Y yields usually mean stronger front-end policy pressure; lower 2Y yields reduce that pressure. The effect is direct for rate assets and indirect for other assets through policy, dollar, and risk channels.
factors.source: U.S. Treasury official Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: 10Y yield
factors.derived: Official Treasury 10 Yr par yield: 4.55% on 2026-07-17
factors.effect: Higher 10Y yields usually increase long-rate pressure and can pressure risk assets and gold; lower 10Y yields usually ease that pressure.
factors.source: U.S. Treasury official Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: Yield curve
factors.derived: Official Treasury curve spreads on 2026-07-17: 10Y-2Y 0.370 percentage points; 10Y-3M 0.700 percentage points.
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on USDCAD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through rates, discount-rate pressure, or funding conditions.
factors.source: U.S. Treasury official Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates

factors.name: Real yields
factors.derived: 10Y TIPS real yield trend and level
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on USDCAD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through rates, discount-rate pressure, or funding conditions.

factors.name: Breakeven inflation
factors.derived: 5Y and 10Y breakeven inflation
factors.effect: Context only: Breakevens affect USDCAD through inflation expectations and real-yield pressure. Use it to confirm or challenge the read, not as the main driver.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: PCE pressure
factors.derived: PCE and Core PCE inflation readings
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Core PCE is important for policy expectations; hotter PCE usually supports rate/yield pressure and can support the dollar side of USDCAD.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: GDP / growth trend
factors.derived: GDP growth, nowcast, or growth surprise vs prior/forecast
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Stronger relative growth can support USDCAD by attracting capital and lifting rate expectations.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Retail sales / consumer demand
factors.derived: Retail sales headline/control group and surprise
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Stronger consumer data can support USDCAD if it lifts growth and rate expectations.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Credit spreads
factors.derived: High-yield and investment-grade spreads vs history
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Credit spreads affect USDCAD through risk appetite and funding-stress conditions.
factors.source: FRED / Federal Reserve public-source candidate with series rights check
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Financial conditions
factors.derived: Financial conditions index or composite of rates, dollar, equities, and credit
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Tighter conditions can support safe-haven currency demand and pressure high-beta FX linked to USDCAD.
factors.source: FRED / Federal Reserve public-source candidate with series rights check
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Open interest
factors.derived: Futures open interest trend and change
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Open interest helps show conviction behind futures positioning for USDCAD. Rising OI with a supportive COT read adds weight; falling OI weakens confirmation.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Crude inventories
factors.derived: Weekly U.S. commercial crude oil stock change
factors.effect: Low direct effect on USDCAD. Watch it only if it spills into energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: Cushing inventories
factors.derived: Cushing crude stock level and weekly change
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on USDCAD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: Product inventories
factors.derived: Gasoline and distillate stock changes
factors.effect: Low direct effect on USDCAD. Watch it only if it spills into energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: Refinery utilization
factors.derived: Percent of refinery capacity utilized
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on USDCAD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: U.S. production
factors.derived: U.S. crude or natural gas production estimate
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on USDCAD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: Imports / exports
factors.derived: Petroleum or commodity trade flow changes
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on USDCAD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: OPEC policy
factors.derived: OPEC official production targets, cuts, increases, compliance notes, and public monthly releases
factors.effect: Low direct effect on USDCAD. Watch it only if it spills into rates, discount-rate pressure, or funding conditions.

factors.name: Stock/use ratio
factors.derived: Ending stocks divided by total use
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on USDCAD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Crop progress / condition
factors.derived: Planting, emergence, harvest progress, and good/excellent crop ratings
factors.effect: Low direct effect on USDCAD. Watch it only if it spills into food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Export sales
factors.derived: Weekly export sales and shipments
factors.effect: Low direct effect on USDCAD. Watch it only if it spills into food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Weather / drought
factors.derived: NOAA/NWS weather, drought monitor, rainfall, heat/freeze risk, and crop-region conditions where available
factors.effect: Low direct effect on USDCAD. Watch it only if it spills into food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Global supply competitors
factors.derived: Brazil/Argentina/Black Sea/EU/Australia supply where relevant
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on USDCAD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Ethanol / biofuel linkage
factors.derived: EIA/USDA ethanol production, corn use for ethanol, and biofuel-demand data
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on USDCAD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: COT spec direction
factors.derived: Traders in Financial Futures - Futures Only for CANADIAN DOLLAR - CHICAGO MERCANTILE EXCHANGE on 2026-07-14: Leveraged Funds long 33,997, short 126,768, net -92,771 (-25.16% of open interest), effective for this asset 25.16%.
factors.effect: Leveraged Funds are net short in the quote-currency futures contract; after USD-base inversion this is supportive for USDCAD. Effective positioning equals 25.16% of open interest. Stronger quote-currency positioning usually pressures a USD-base pair.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: COT spec weekly change
factors.derived: Weekly net change -6,814; effective weekly change 1.85% of open interest.
factors.effect: Weekly change shows whether speculative participation is getting stronger or weaker for U.S. Dollar / Canadian Dollar. Adding exposure in the supportive direction strengthens the positioning read; reducing exposure weakens it.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: COT crowding risk
factors.derived: Spec effective positioning percentile over recent lookback: 77.40%.
factors.effect: Spec positioning is not at a recent extreme, so crowding risk is not a major signal yet.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

Weekly change: -2,976 thousand barrels. | U.S. motor gasoline stocks (W_EPM0_SAX_NUS_MBBL) latest missing thousand barrels on None; previous missing thousand barrels on None. | U.S. distillate fuel oil stocks (W_EPD0_SAX_NUS_MBBL) latest missing thousand barrels on None; previous missing thousand barrels on None.
factors.effect: Contextual effect: stronger oil/energy fundamentals can support CAD through Canada's commodity linkage; because this pair is USD/CAD, CAD support can pressure the pair.
factors.source: U.S. Energy Information Administration open data/API

scoreChangeLog.summary: USDCAD is broadly stable versus the previous saved snapshot.

assets.name: Australian Dollar / U.S. Dollar
assets.mainConflict: St. Louis financial stress (fed, +0.97) vs BLS CPI energy pressure (bls, -1.60)
assets.quick: Live-only scoring shows negative raw pressure from 25 counted rows. Top driver: BLS CPI energy pressure (bls, -1.60). Raw score is uncapped, source-weighted, and excludes non-live rows.

factors.name: Policy rate
factors.derived: Current central bank target rate or policy band
factors.effect: Primary effect: Higher relative policy rates usually support AUDUSD; lower or dovish policy usually pressures it.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Rate differentials
factors.derived: Relevant yield spread between countries or curves, usually 2Y and 10Y comparisons
factors.effect: Primary effect: Wider yield spreads in favor of AUDUSD usually support the currency pair; narrowing spreads usually pressure it.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Real yield differentials
factors.derived: Inflation-adjusted yield spread, often 10Y real yield vs peer real yield
factors.effect: Primary effect: Wider inflation-adjusted yield advantage usually supports AUDUSD; a weaker real-yield spread usually pressures it.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: 2Y yield
factors.derived: Official Treasury 2 Yr par yield: 4.18% on 2026-07-17
factors.effect: Higher 2Y yields usually mean stronger front-end policy pressure; lower 2Y yields reduce that pressure. The effect is direct for rate assets and indirect for other assets through policy, dollar, and risk channels.
factors.source: U.S. Treasury official Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: 10Y yield
factors.derived: Official Treasury 10 Yr par yield: 4.55% on 2026-07-17
factors.effect: Higher 10Y yields usually increase long-rate pressure and can pressure risk assets and gold; lower 10Y yields usually ease that pressure.
factors.source: U.S. Treasury official Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: Yield curve
factors.derived: Official Treasury curve spreads on 2026-07-17: 10Y-2Y 0.370 percentage points; 10Y-3M 0.700 percentage points.
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on AUDUSD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through rates, discount-rate pressure, or funding conditions.
factors.source: U.S. Treasury official Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates

factors.name: Real yields
factors.derived: 10Y TIPS real yield trend and level
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on AUDUSD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through rates, discount-rate pressure, or funding conditions.

factors.name: Breakeven inflation
factors.derived: 5Y and 10Y breakeven inflation
factors.effect: Context only: Breakevens affect AUDUSD through inflation expectations and real-yield pressure. Use it to confirm or challenge the read, not as the main driver.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: PCE pressure
factors.derived: PCE and Core PCE inflation readings
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Core PCE is important for policy expectations; hotter PCE usually supports rate/yield pressure and can support the dollar side of AUDUSD.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: GDP / growth trend
factors.derived: GDP growth, nowcast, or growth surprise vs prior/forecast
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Stronger relative growth can support AUDUSD by attracting capital and lifting rate expectations.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Retail sales / consumer demand
factors.derived: Retail sales headline/control group and surprise
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Stronger consumer data can support AUDUSD if it lifts growth and rate expectations.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Credit spreads
factors.derived: High-yield and investment-grade spreads vs history
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Credit spreads affect AUDUSD through risk appetite and funding-stress conditions.
factors.source: FRED / Federal Reserve public-source candidate with series rights check
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Financial conditions
factors.derived: Financial conditions index or composite of rates, dollar, equities, and credit
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Tighter conditions can support safe-haven currency demand and pressure high-beta FX linked to AUDUSD.
factors.source: FRED / Federal Reserve public-source candidate with series rights check
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Open interest
factors.derived: Futures open interest trend and change
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Open interest helps show conviction behind futures positioning for AUDUSD. Rising OI with a supportive COT read adds weight; falling OI weakens confirmation.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: COT spec direction
factors.derived: Traders in Financial Futures - Futures Only for AUSTRALIAN DOLLAR - CHICAGO MERCANTILE EXCHANGE on 2026-07-14: Leveraged Funds long 55,916, short 28,694, net 27,222 (13.06% of open interest), effective for this asset 13.06%.
factors.effect: Leveraged Funds are net long in the related currency futures contract, which is supportive for this FX read. Effective positioning equals 13.06% of open interest. This reflects speculative participation, not a timing signal.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: COT spec weekly change
factors.derived: Weekly net change -2,461; effective weekly change -1.18% of open interest.
factors.effect: Weekly change shows whether speculative participation is getting stronger or weaker for Australian Dollar / U.S. Dollar. Adding exposure in the supportive direction strengthens the positioning read; reducing exposure weakens it.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: COT crowding risk
factors.derived: Spec effective positioning percentile over recent lookback: 45.30%.
factors.effect: Spec positioning is not at a recent extreme, so crowding risk is not a major signal yet.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

scoreChangeLog.summary: AUDUSD moved +2.1 points versus the previous saved snapshot; current primary state is Moderate negative pressure.

assets.name: New Zealand Dollar / U.S. Dollar
assets.mainConflict: St. Louis financial stress (fed, +0.97) vs BLS CPI energy pressure (bls, -1.60)
assets.quick: Live-only scoring shows negative raw pressure from 25 counted rows. Top driver: BLS CPI energy pressure (bls, -1.60). Raw score is uncapped, source-weighted, and excludes non-live rows.

factors.name: Policy rate
factors.derived: Current central bank target rate or policy band
factors.effect: Primary effect: Higher relative policy rates usually support NZDUSD; lower or dovish policy usually pressures it.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Rate differentials
factors.derived: Relevant yield spread between countries or curves, usually 2Y and 10Y comparisons
factors.effect: Primary effect: Wider yield spreads in favor of NZDUSD usually support the currency pair; narrowing spreads usually pressure it.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Real yield differentials
factors.derived: Inflation-adjusted yield spread, often 10Y real yield vs peer real yield
factors.effect: Primary effect: Wider inflation-adjusted yield advantage usually supports NZDUSD; a weaker real-yield spread usually pressures it.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: 2Y yield
factors.derived: Official Treasury 2 Yr par yield: 4.18% on 2026-07-17
factors.effect: Higher 2Y yields usually mean stronger front-end policy pressure; lower 2Y yields reduce that pressure. The effect is direct for rate assets and indirect for other assets through policy, dollar, and risk channels.
factors.source: U.S. Treasury official Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: 10Y yield
factors.derived: Official Treasury 10 Yr par yield: 4.55% on 2026-07-17
factors.effect: Higher 10Y yields usually increase long-rate pressure and can pressure risk assets and gold; lower 10Y yields usually ease that pressure.
factors.source: U.S. Treasury official Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: Yield curve
factors.derived: Official Treasury curve spreads on 2026-07-17: 10Y-2Y 0.370 percentage points; 10Y-3M 0.700 percentage points.
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on NZDUSD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through rates, discount-rate pressure, or funding conditions.
factors.source: U.S. Treasury official Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates

factors.name: Real yields
factors.derived: 10Y TIPS real yield trend and level
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on NZDUSD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through rates, discount-rate pressure, or funding conditions.

factors.name: Breakeven inflation
factors.derived: 5Y and 10Y breakeven inflation
factors.effect: Context only: Breakevens affect NZDUSD through inflation expectations and real-yield pressure. Use it to confirm or challenge the read, not as the main driver.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: PCE pressure
factors.derived: PCE and Core PCE inflation readings
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Core PCE is important for policy expectations; hotter PCE usually supports rate/yield pressure and can support the dollar side of NZDUSD.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: GDP / growth trend
factors.derived: GDP growth, nowcast, or growth surprise vs prior/forecast
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Stronger relative growth can support NZDUSD by attracting capital and lifting rate expectations.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Retail sales / consumer demand
factors.derived: Retail sales headline/control group and surprise
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Stronger consumer data can support NZDUSD if it lifts growth and rate expectations.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Credit spreads
factors.derived: High-yield and investment-grade spreads vs history
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Credit spreads affect NZDUSD through risk appetite and funding-stress conditions.
factors.source: FRED / Federal Reserve public-source candidate with series rights check
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Financial conditions
factors.derived: Financial conditions index or composite of rates, dollar, equities, and credit
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Tighter conditions can support safe-haven currency demand and pressure high-beta FX linked to NZDUSD.
factors.source: FRED / Federal Reserve public-source candidate with series rights check
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Open interest
factors.derived: Futures open interest trend and change
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Open interest helps show conviction behind futures positioning for NZDUSD. Rising OI with a supportive COT read adds weight; falling OI weakens confirmation.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.effect: Low direct effect on NZDUSD. Watch it only if it spills into energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.effect: Low direct effect on NZDUSD. Watch it only if it spills into energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on NZDUSD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on NZDUSD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on NZDUSD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.effect: Low direct effect on NZDUSD. Watch it only if it spills into rates, discount-rate pressure, or funding conditions.

factors.name: LNG exports / power burn

factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on NZDUSD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.derived: USDA WASDE ending stocks for wheat, corn, soybeans, cotton
factors.effect: Low direct effect on NZDUSD. Watch it only if it spills into food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Stock/use ratio
factors.derived: Ending stocks divided by total use
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on NZDUSD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Crop progress / condition
factors.derived: Planting, emergence, harvest progress, and good/excellent crop ratings
factors.effect: Low direct effect on NZDUSD. Watch it only if it spills into food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Export sales
factors.derived: Weekly export sales and shipments
factors.effect: Low direct effect on NZDUSD. Watch it only if it spills into food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Weather / drought
factors.derived: NOAA/NWS weather, drought monitor, rainfall, heat/freeze risk, and crop-region conditions where available
factors.effect: Low direct effect on NZDUSD. Watch it only if it spills into food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Global supply competitors
factors.derived: Brazil/Argentina/Black Sea/EU/Australia supply where relevant
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on NZDUSD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Ethanol / biofuel linkage
factors.derived: EIA/USDA ethanol production, corn use for ethanol, and biofuel-demand data
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on NZDUSD. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: COT spec direction
factors.derived: CFTC COT net speculative positioning for the mapped futures contract; live values appear after Refresh CFTC COT Lane runs.
factors.effect: Spec direction shows trend participation. Net long speculative exposure can support the asset; net short exposure can pressure it, with inversion applied for Treasury yield assets and USD-base FX pairs.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports

factors.name: COT spec weekly change
factors.derived: Weekly change in speculative net exposure from the CFTC COT report.
factors.effect: Weekly change shows whether specs are adding to or reducing the directional positioning read. Adding exposure in the supportive direction strengthens the signal; reducing exposure weakens it.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports

factors.name: COT crowding risk
factors.derived: Recent percentile/range position of speculative net exposure where available.
factors.effect: Crowding risk separates participation from fragility. Stretched long positioning can create unwind risk; stretched short positioning can create short-covering risk.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports

factors.name: COT commercial / hedger extreme
factors.derived: Producer/merchant/processor/user positioning for commodity futures when available.
factors.effect: Commercial/hedger positioning is not an automatic signal. Extreme commercial long exposure can suggest value/support context; extreme commercial short exposure can warn that speculative long exposure may be crowded.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports

factors.name: COT spec-commercial conflict
factors.derived: Comparison of speculative positioning against commercial/hedger positioning where available.
factors.effect: Conflict means specs and commercials are meaningfully opposed. The trend participation read can still work, but reversal or unwind risk deserves more attention.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports

assets.name: U.S. 2-Year Treasury Yield
assets.mainConflict: BLS CPI energy pressure (bls, +2.10) vs Census building permits (census, -1.10)
assets.quick: Live-only scoring shows positive raw pressure from 31 counted rows. Top driver: BLS CPI energy pressure (bls, +2.10). Raw score is uncapped, source-weighted, and excludes non-live rows.

factors.name: Policy rate
factors.derived: Current central bank target rate or policy band
factors.effect: Primary effect: Higher policy-rate expectations usually push yield pressure higher for 2Y; easier policy usually lowers rate pressure.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Rate differentials
factors.derived: Relevant yield spread between countries or curves, usually 2Y and 10Y comparisons
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 2Y. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through rates, discount-rate pressure, or funding conditions.

factors.name: Real yield differentials
factors.derived: Inflation-adjusted yield spread, often 10Y real yield vs peer real yield
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 2Y. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through rates, discount-rate pressure, or funding conditions.

factors.name: 2Y yield
factors.derived: Official Treasury 2 Yr par yield: 4.18% on 2026-07-17; previous 4.16% on 2026-07-16
factors.effect: For the U.S. 2-Year Treasury Yield, this official yield is the direct input. Higher yields mean stronger rate pressure; lower yields mean weaker rate pressure. This is a public-source rates input, not a trade-timing signal.
factors.source: U.S. Treasury official Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates

factors.name: 10Y yield
factors.derived: 10-year government bond yield trend and level
factors.effect: Primary effect: The 10Y yield is the direct macro-rate input for 2Y; higher readings mean more long-rate pressure.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Yield curve
factors.derived: 2Y minus 10Y, 5Y minus 30Y, or comparable curve slope
factors.effect: Primary effect: The curve is a direct regime input for 2Y; steepening can imply changing growth/inflation expectations, while flattening can warn of restrictive policy or growth stress.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Real yields
factors.derived: 10Y TIPS real yield trend and level
factors.effect: Primary effect: Real yields affect 2Y through inflation-adjusted return and discount-rate pressure.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Breakeven inflation
factors.derived: 5Y and 10Y breakeven inflation
factors.effect: Primary effect: Breakevens affect 2Y through inflation expectations and real-yield pressure.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: PCE pressure
factors.derived: PCE and Core PCE inflation readings
factors.effect: Primary effect: Core PCE is important for policy expectations; hotter PCE usually supports rate/yield pressure and can support the dollar side of 2Y.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: GDP / growth trend
factors.derived: GDP growth, nowcast, or growth surprise vs prior/forecast
factors.effect: Primary effect: Stronger growth usually raises yield pressure for 2Y; weaker growth usually lowers yields and raises slowdown risk.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Retail sales / consumer demand
factors.derived: Retail sales headline/control group and surprise
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Consumer demand affects 2Y through growth expectations and policy reaction.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Credit spreads
factors.derived: High-yield and investment-grade spreads vs history
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Credit spreads affect 2Y through risk appetite and funding-stress conditions.
factors.source: FRED / Federal Reserve public-source candidate with series rights check
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Financial conditions
factors.derived: Financial conditions index or composite of rates, dollar, equities, and credit
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Financial conditions affect 2Y through liquidity, credit availability, and risk appetite.
factors.source: FRED / Federal Reserve public-source candidate with series rights check
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Open interest
factors.derived: Futures open interest trend and change
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Open interest helps show conviction behind futures positioning for 2Y. Rising OI with a supportive COT read adds weight; falling OI weakens confirmation.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Crude inventories
factors.derived: Weekly U.S. commercial crude oil stock change
factors.effect: Context only: Crude inventory draws usually support oil-linked assets by signaling tighter supply; builds usually pressure them. Use it to confirm or challenge the read, not as the main driver.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Cushing inventories

factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 2Y. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: Product inventories

factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 2Y. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: Refinery utilization

factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 2Y. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 2Y. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: U.S. production

factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 2Y. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: Imports / exports
factors.derived: Petroleum or commodity trade flow changes
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 2Y. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: OPEC policy

factors.effect: Context only: OPEC cuts usually support oil by reducing supply; production increases or weak compliance usually pressure the oil balance. Use it to confirm or challenge the read, not as the main driver.

factors.effect: Low direct effect on 2Y. Watch it only if it spills into food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.effect: Low direct effect on 2Y. Watch it only if it spills into food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: COT spec direction
factors.derived: Traders in Financial Futures - Futures Only for UST 2Y NOTE - CHICAGO BOARD OF TRADE on 2026-07-14: Leveraged Funds long 389,534, short 2,061,300, net -1,671,766 (-37.44% of open interest), effective for this asset 37.44%.
factors.effect: Leveraged Funds are net short in the related Treasury futures contract; after yield inversion this is supportive for U.S. 2-Year Treasury Yield. Effective positioning equals 37.44% of open interest. Long Treasury futures usually imply bond-price support and lower-yield pressure.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: COT spec weekly change
factors.derived: Weekly net change 84,762; effective weekly change -1.90% of open interest.
factors.effect: Weekly change shows whether speculative participation is getting stronger or weaker for U.S. 2-Year Treasury Yield. Adding exposure in the supportive direction strengthens the positioning read; reducing exposure weakens it.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: COT crowding risk
factors.derived: Spec effective positioning percentile over recent lookback: 3.80%.
factors.effect: Spec positioning is not at a recent extreme, so crowding risk is not a major signal yet.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold
