scoreChangeLog.summary: 2Y is broadly stable versus the previous saved snapshot.

assets.name: U.S. 5-Year Treasury Yield
assets.mainConflict: BLS CPI energy pressure (bls, +2.10) vs Census building permits (census, -1.10)
assets.quick: Live-only scoring shows positive raw pressure from 31 counted rows. Top driver: BLS CPI energy pressure (bls, +2.10). Raw score is uncapped, source-weighted, and excludes non-live rows.

factors.name: 5Y yield
factors.derived: Official Treasury 5 Yr par yield: 4.28% on 2026-07-17; previous 4.28% on 2026-07-16
factors.effect: For the U.S. 5-Year Treasury Yield, this official yield is the direct input. Higher yields mean stronger rate pressure; lower yields mean weaker rate pressure. This is a public-source rates input, not a trade-timing signal.
factors.source: U.S. Treasury official Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates

factors.name: Policy rate
factors.derived: Current central bank target rate or policy band
factors.effect: Primary effect: Higher policy-rate expectations usually push yield pressure higher for 5Y; easier policy usually lowers rate pressure.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Rate differentials
factors.derived: Relevant yield spread between countries or curves, usually 2Y and 10Y comparisons
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 5Y. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through rates, discount-rate pressure, or funding conditions.

factors.name: Real yield differentials
factors.derived: Inflation-adjusted yield spread, often 10Y real yield vs peer real yield
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 5Y. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through rates, discount-rate pressure, or funding conditions.

factors.name: 2Y yield
factors.derived: 2-year government bond yield trend and level
factors.effect: Primary effect: The 2Y yield is a direct policy-expectations input for 5Y; higher 2Y yield means stronger front-end rate pressure.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: 10Y yield
factors.derived: 10-year government bond yield trend and level
factors.effect: Primary effect: The 10Y yield is the direct macro-rate input for 5Y; higher readings mean more long-rate pressure.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Yield curve
factors.derived: 2Y minus 10Y, 5Y minus 30Y, or comparable curve slope
factors.effect: Primary effect: The curve is a direct regime input for 5Y; steepening can imply changing growth/inflation expectations, while flattening can warn of restrictive policy or growth stress.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Real yields
factors.derived: 10Y TIPS real yield trend and level
factors.effect: Primary effect: Real yields affect 5Y through inflation-adjusted return and discount-rate pressure.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Breakeven inflation
factors.derived: 5Y and 10Y breakeven inflation
factors.effect: Primary effect: Breakevens affect 5Y through inflation expectations and real-yield pressure.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: PCE pressure
factors.derived: PCE and Core PCE inflation readings
factors.effect: Primary effect: Core PCE is important for policy expectations; hotter PCE usually supports rate/yield pressure and can support the dollar side of 5Y.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: GDP / growth trend
factors.derived: GDP growth, nowcast, or growth surprise vs prior/forecast
factors.effect: Primary effect: Stronger growth usually raises yield pressure for 5Y; weaker growth usually lowers yields and raises slowdown risk.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Retail sales / consumer demand
factors.derived: Retail sales headline/control group and surprise
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Consumer demand affects 5Y through growth expectations and policy reaction.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Credit spreads
factors.derived: High-yield and investment-grade spreads vs history
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Credit spreads affect 5Y through risk appetite and funding-stress conditions.
factors.source: FRED / Federal Reserve public-source candidate with series rights check
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Financial conditions
factors.derived: Financial conditions index or composite of rates, dollar, equities, and credit
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Financial conditions affect 5Y through liquidity, credit availability, and risk appetite.
factors.source: FRED / Federal Reserve public-source candidate with series rights check
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Open interest
factors.derived: Futures open interest trend and change
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Open interest helps show conviction behind futures positioning for 5Y. Rising OI with a supportive COT read adds weight; falling OI weakens confirmation.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Crude inventories
factors.derived: Weekly U.S. commercial crude oil stock change
factors.effect: Context only: Crude inventory draws usually support oil-linked assets by signaling tighter supply; builds usually pressure them. Use it to confirm or challenge the read, not as the main driver.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: COT spec direction
factors.derived: Traders in Financial Futures - Futures Only for UST 5Y NOTE - CHICAGO BOARD OF TRADE on 2026-07-14: Leveraged Funds long 387,093, short 2,543,843, net -2,156,750 (-34.89% of open interest), effective for this asset 34.89%.
factors.effect: Leveraged Funds are net short in the related Treasury futures contract; after yield inversion this is supportive for U.S. 5-Year Treasury Yield. Effective positioning equals 34.89% of open interest. Long Treasury futures usually imply bond-price support and lower-yield pressure.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: COT spec weekly change
factors.derived: Weekly net change 18,112; effective weekly change -0.29% of open interest.
factors.effect: Weekly change shows whether speculative participation is getting stronger or weaker for U.S. 5-Year Treasury Yield. Adding exposure in the supportive direction strengthens the positioning read; reducing exposure weakens it.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: COT crowding risk
factors.derived: Spec effective positioning percentile over recent lookback: 11.30%.
factors.effect: Spec positioning is not at a recent extreme, so crowding risk is not a major signal yet.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

scoreChangeLog.summary: 5Y is broadly stable versus the previous saved snapshot.

assets.name: U.S. 10-Year Treasury Yield
assets.mainConflict: BLS CPI energy pressure (bls, +2.10) vs Census building permits (census, -1.10)
assets.quick: Live-only scoring shows positive raw pressure from 31 counted rows. Top driver: BLS CPI energy pressure (bls, +2.10). Raw score is uncapped, source-weighted, and excludes non-live rows.

factors.name: Policy rate
factors.derived: Current central bank target rate or policy band
factors.effect: Primary effect: Higher policy-rate expectations usually push yield pressure higher for 10Y; easier policy usually lowers rate pressure.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Rate differentials
factors.derived: Relevant yield spread between countries or curves, usually 2Y and 10Y comparisons
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 10Y. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through rates, discount-rate pressure, or funding conditions.

factors.name: Real yield differentials
factors.derived: Inflation-adjusted yield spread, often 10Y real yield vs peer real yield
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 10Y. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through rates, discount-rate pressure, or funding conditions.

factors.name: 2Y yield
factors.derived: 2-year government bond yield trend and level
factors.effect: Primary effect: The 2Y yield is a direct policy-expectations input for 10Y; higher 2Y yield means stronger front-end rate pressure.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: 10Y yield
factors.derived: Official Treasury 10 Yr par yield: 4.55% on 2026-07-17; previous 4.57% on 2026-07-16
factors.effect: For the U.S. 10-Year Treasury Yield, this official yield is the direct input. Higher yields mean stronger rate pressure; lower yields mean weaker rate pressure. This is a public-source rates input, not a trade-timing signal.
factors.source: U.S. Treasury official Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates

factors.name: Yield curve
factors.derived: 2Y minus 10Y, 5Y minus 30Y, or comparable curve slope
factors.effect: Primary effect: The curve is a direct regime input for 10Y; steepening can imply changing growth/inflation expectations, while flattening can warn of restrictive policy or growth stress.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Real yields
factors.derived: 10Y TIPS real yield trend and level
factors.effect: Primary effect: Real yields affect 10Y through inflation-adjusted return and discount-rate pressure.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Breakeven inflation
factors.derived: 5Y and 10Y breakeven inflation
factors.effect: Primary effect: Breakevens affect 10Y through inflation expectations and real-yield pressure.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: PCE pressure
factors.derived: PCE and Core PCE inflation readings
factors.effect: Primary effect: Core PCE is important for policy expectations; hotter PCE usually supports rate/yield pressure and can support the dollar side of 10Y.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: GDP / growth trend
factors.derived: GDP growth, nowcast, or growth surprise vs prior/forecast
factors.effect: Primary effect: Stronger growth usually raises yield pressure for 10Y; weaker growth usually lowers yields and raises slowdown risk.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Retail sales / consumer demand
factors.derived: Retail sales headline/control group and surprise
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Consumer demand affects 10Y through growth expectations and policy reaction.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Credit spreads
factors.derived: High-yield and investment-grade spreads vs history
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Credit spreads affect 10Y through risk appetite and funding-stress conditions.
factors.source: FRED / Federal Reserve public-source candidate with series rights check
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Financial conditions
factors.derived: Financial conditions index or composite of rates, dollar, equities, and credit
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Financial conditions affect 10Y through liquidity, credit availability, and risk appetite.
factors.source: FRED / Federal Reserve public-source candidate with series rights check
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Open interest
factors.derived: Futures open interest trend and change
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Open interest helps show conviction behind futures positioning for 10Y. Rising OI with a supportive COT read adds weight; falling OI weakens confirmation.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Crude inventories
factors.derived: Weekly U.S. commercial crude oil stock change
factors.effect: Context only: Crude inventory draws usually support oil-linked assets by signaling tighter supply; builds usually pressure them. Use it to confirm or challenge the read, not as the main driver.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Cushing inventories

factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 10Y. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: Product inventories

factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 10Y. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: Refinery utilization

factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 10Y. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: Product supplied / demand

factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 10Y. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: U.S. production
factors.derived: U.S. crude or natural gas production estimate
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 10Y. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: Imports / exports
factors.derived: Petroleum or commodity trade flow changes
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 10Y. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: OPEC policy
factors.derived: OPEC official production targets, cuts, increases, compliance notes, and public monthly releases
factors.effect: Context only: OPEC cuts usually support oil by reducing supply; production increases or weak compliance usually pressure the oil balance. Use it to confirm or challenge the read, not as the main driver.

factors.effect: Low direct effect on 10Y. Watch it only if it spills into food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.effect: Low direct effect on 10Y. Watch it only if it spills into food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: COT spec direction
factors.derived: Traders in Financial Futures - Futures Only for UST 10Y NOTE - CHICAGO BOARD OF TRADE on 2026-07-14: Leveraged Funds long 387,963, short 2,467,616, net -2,079,653 (-39.35% of open interest), effective for this asset 39.35%.
factors.effect: Leveraged Funds are net short in the related Treasury futures contract; after yield inversion this is supportive for U.S. 10-Year Treasury Yield. Effective positioning equals 39.35% of open interest. Long Treasury futures usually imply bond-price support and lower-yield pressure.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: COT spec weekly change
factors.derived: Weekly net change -75,630; effective weekly change 1.43% of open interest.
factors.effect: Weekly change shows whether speculative participation is getting stronger or weaker for U.S. 10-Year Treasury Yield. Adding exposure in the supportive direction strengthens the positioning read; reducing exposure weakens it.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: COT crowding risk
factors.derived: Spec effective positioning percentile over recent lookback: 50.90%.
factors.effect: Spec positioning is not at a recent extreme, so crowding risk is not a major signal yet.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.effect: Secondary effect: Credit-spread stress can reduce growth expectations and lower upward yield pressure for U.S. 10-Year Treasury Yield, even while risk premiums rise.
factors.source: Federal Reserve / FRED credit and financial-stress public data

factors.name: National financial conditions
factors.derived: Chicago Fed National Financial Conditions Index (NFCI) latest -0.54 index for 2026-07-10; previous -0.52 index for 2026-07-03; change -0.01 pts. Raw lane score uses easier/lower-stress conditions as positive and tighter/higher-stress conditions as negative.
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Financial stress can reduce growth/rate pressure for U.S. 10-Year Treasury Yield; easier conditions can support risk-taking and rate pressure.
factors.source: Federal Reserve / FRED credit and financial-stress public data

factors.name: Adjusted financial conditions
factors.derived: Chicago Fed Adjusted National Financial Conditions Index (ANFCI) latest -0.54 index for 2026-07-10; previous -0.52 index for 2026-07-03; change -0.02 pts. Raw lane score uses easier/lower-stress conditions as positive and tighter/higher-stress conditions as negative.
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Financial stress can reduce growth/rate pressure for U.S. 10-Year Treasury Yield; easier conditions can support risk-taking and rate pressure.
factors.source: Federal Reserve / FRED credit and financial-stress public data

factors.name: St. Louis financial stress
factors.derived: St. Louis Fed Financial Stress Index (STLFSI4) latest -0.88 index for 2026-07-10; previous -0.72 index for 2026-07-03; change -0.16 pts. Raw lane score uses easier/lower-stress conditions as positive and tighter/higher-stress conditions as negative.
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Financial stress can reduce growth/rate pressure for U.S. 10-Year Treasury Yield; easier conditions can support risk-taking and rate pressure.
factors.source: Federal Reserve / FRED credit and financial-stress public data

factors.name: NOAA active weather hazards
factors.derived: NOAA/NWS active weather alerts (NWS_ACTIVE_ALERTS) latest 486 active alerts for 2026-07-18; release/effective date 2026-07-17T21:03:00-04:00. Raw lane score treats low active hazard load as positive and elevated weather disruption as negative. Sample events: Air Quality Alert, Small Craft Advisory, Special Weather Statement, Gale Warning, Flood Advisory.
factors.effect: Contextual effect: Weather hazards usually affect U.S. 10-Year Treasury Yield indirectly through temporary growth, energy-demand, inflation, and disruption channels.
factors.source: NOAA/National Weather Service public weather alerts

factors.name: NOAA winter storm stress
factors.derived: NOAA winter storm alerts (NWS_WINTER_STORM_STRESS) latest 0 active alerts for 2026-07-18; weighted count 0; release/effective date 2026-07-17T21:03:00-04:00. Raw lane score treats low active hazard load as positive and elevated weather disruption as negative.

Sample events: Flash Flood Warning, Flash Flood Warning, Flood Advisory, Flood Advisory, Flood Advisory.
factors.effect: Contextual effect: Weather hazards usually affect U.S. 10-Year Treasury Yield indirectly through temporary growth, energy-demand, inflation, and disruption channels.

factors.derived: NOAA tropical storm / hurricane alerts (NWS_TROPICAL_STORM_STRESS) latest 0 active alerts for 2026-07-18; weighted count 0; release/effective date 2026-07-17T21:03:00-04:00. Raw lane score treats low active hazard load as positive and elevated weather disruption as negative.

scoreChangeLog.summary: 10Y is broadly stable versus the previous saved snapshot.

assets.name: U.S. 30-Year Treasury Yield
assets.mainConflict: BLS CPI energy pressure (bls, +2.10) vs 30Y yield (treasury, -1.30)
assets.quick: Live-only scoring shows positive raw pressure from 31 counted rows. Top driver: BLS CPI energy pressure (bls, +2.10). Raw score is uncapped, source-weighted, and excludes non-live rows.

factors.name: 30Y yield
factors.derived: Official Treasury 30 Yr par yield: 5.06% on 2026-07-17; previous 5.09% on 2026-07-16
factors.effect: For the U.S. 30-Year Treasury Yield, this official yield is the direct input. Higher yields mean stronger rate pressure; lower yields mean weaker rate pressure. This is a public-source rates input, not a trade-timing signal.
factors.source: U.S. Treasury official Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates

factors.name: Policy rate
factors.derived: Current central bank target rate or policy band
factors.effect: Primary effect: Higher policy-rate expectations usually push yield pressure higher for 30Y; easier policy usually lowers rate pressure.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Rate differentials
factors.derived: Relevant yield spread between countries or curves, usually 2Y and 10Y comparisons
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 30Y. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through rates, discount-rate pressure, or funding conditions.

factors.name: Real yield differentials
factors.derived: Inflation-adjusted yield spread, often 10Y real yield vs peer real yield
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 30Y. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through rates, discount-rate pressure, or funding conditions.

factors.name: 2Y yield
factors.derived: 2-year government bond yield trend and level
factors.effect: Primary effect: The 2Y yield is a direct policy-expectations input for 30Y; higher 2Y yield means stronger front-end rate pressure.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: 10Y yield
factors.derived: 10-year government bond yield trend and level
factors.effect: Primary effect: The 10Y yield is the direct macro-rate input for 30Y; higher readings mean more long-rate pressure.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Yield curve
factors.derived: 2Y minus 10Y, 5Y minus 30Y, or comparable curve slope
factors.effect: Primary effect: The curve is a direct regime input for 30Y; steepening can imply changing growth/inflation expectations, while flattening can warn of restrictive policy or growth stress.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Real yields
factors.derived: 10Y TIPS real yield trend and level
factors.effect: Primary effect: Real yields affect 30Y through inflation-adjusted return and discount-rate pressure.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Breakeven inflation
factors.derived: 5Y and 10Y breakeven inflation
factors.effect: Primary effect: Breakevens affect 30Y through inflation expectations and real-yield pressure.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: PCE pressure
factors.derived: PCE and Core PCE inflation readings
factors.effect: Primary effect: Core PCE is important for policy expectations; hotter PCE usually supports rate/yield pressure and can support the dollar side of 30Y.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: GDP / growth trend
factors.derived: GDP growth, nowcast, or growth surprise vs prior/forecast
factors.effect: Primary effect: Stronger growth usually raises yield pressure for 30Y; weaker growth usually lowers yields and raises slowdown risk.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Retail sales / consumer demand
factors.derived: Retail sales headline/control group and surprise
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Consumer demand affects 30Y through growth expectations and policy reaction.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Credit spreads
factors.derived: High-yield and investment-grade spreads vs history
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Credit spreads affect 30Y through risk appetite and funding-stress conditions.
factors.source: FRED / Federal Reserve public-source candidate with series rights check
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Financial conditions
factors.derived: Financial conditions index or composite of rates, dollar, equities, and credit
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Financial conditions affect 30Y through liquidity, credit availability, and risk appetite.
factors.source: FRED / Federal Reserve public-source candidate with series rights check
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Open interest
factors.derived: Futures open interest trend and change
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Open interest helps show conviction behind futures positioning for 30Y. Rising OI with a supportive COT read adds weight; falling OI weakens confirmation.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Crude inventories
factors.derived: Weekly U.S. commercial crude oil stock change
factors.effect: Context only: Crude inventory draws usually support oil-linked assets by signaling tighter supply; builds usually pressure them. Use it to confirm or challenge the read, not as the main driver.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Cushing inventories
factors.derived: Cushing crude stock level and weekly change
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 30Y. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: Product inventories
factors.derived: Gasoline and distillate stock changes
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 30Y. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: Refinery utilization

factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 30Y. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: Product supplied / demand

factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 30Y. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: U.S. production
factors.derived: U.S. crude or natural gas production estimate
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 30Y. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: Imports / exports
factors.derived: Petroleum or commodity trade flow changes
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 30Y. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: OPEC policy
factors.derived: OPEC official production targets, cuts, increases, compliance notes, and public monthly releases
factors.effect: Context only: OPEC cuts usually support oil by reducing supply; production increases or weak compliance usually pressure the oil balance. Use it to confirm or challenge the read, not as the main driver.

factors.derived: USDA WASDE ending stocks for wheat, corn, soybeans, cotton
factors.effect: Low direct effect on 30Y. Watch it only if it spills into food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Stock/use ratio
factors.derived: Ending stocks divided by total use
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 30Y. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Crop progress / condition
factors.derived: Planting, emergence, harvest progress, and good/excellent crop ratings
factors.effect: Low direct effect on 30Y. Watch it only if it spills into food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Export sales
factors.derived: Weekly export sales and shipments
factors.effect: Low direct effect on 30Y. Watch it only if it spills into food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Weather / drought
factors.derived: NOAA/NWS weather, drought monitor, rainfall, heat/freeze risk, and crop-region conditions where available
factors.effect: Low direct effect on 30Y. Watch it only if it spills into food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Global supply competitors

factors.name: Ethanol / biofuel linkage

factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 30Y. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: COT spec direction
factors.derived: Traders in Financial Futures - Futures Only for ULTRA UST BOND - CHICAGO BOARD OF TRADE on 2026-07-14: Leveraged Funds long 69,506, short 979,958, net -910,452 (-37.05% of open interest), effective for this asset 37.05%.
factors.effect: Leveraged Funds are net short in the related Treasury futures contract; after yield inversion this is supportive for U.S. 30-Year Treasury Yield. Effective positioning equals 37.05% of open interest. Long Treasury futures usually imply bond-price support and lower-yield pressure.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: COT spec weekly change
factors.derived: Weekly net change 10,196; effective weekly change -0.41% of open interest.
factors.effect: Weekly change shows whether speculative participation is getting stronger or weaker for U.S. 30-Year Treasury Yield. Adding exposure in the supportive direction strengthens the positioning read; reducing exposure weakens it.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.name: COT crowding risk
factors.derived: Spec effective positioning percentile over recent lookback: 72.50%.
factors.effect: Spec positioning is not at a recent extreme, so crowding risk is not a major signal yet.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports
factors.scoreReason: live context row, below scoring threshold

factors.effect: Secondary effect: Credit-spread stress can reduce growth expectations and lower upward yield pressure for U.S. 30-Year Treasury Yield, even while risk premiums rise.

scoreChangeLog.summary: 30Y is broadly stable versus the previous saved snapshot.

assets.name: U.S. 2s10s Yield Curve
assets.mainConflict: BLS CPI energy pressure (bls, +1.40) vs Yield curve (treasury, -1.25)
assets.quick: Live-only scoring shows positive raw pressure from 26 counted rows. Top driver: BLS CPI energy pressure (bls, +1.40). Raw score is uncapped, source-weighted, and excludes non-live rows.

factors.name: Policy rate
factors.derived: Current central bank target rate or policy band
factors.effect: Primary effect: Higher policy-rate expectations usually push yield pressure higher for 2s10s; easier policy usually lowers rate pressure.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Rate differentials
factors.derived: Relevant yield spread between countries or curves, usually 2Y and 10Y comparisons
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 2s10s. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through rates, discount-rate pressure, or funding conditions.

factors.name: Real yield differentials
factors.derived: Inflation-adjusted yield spread, often 10Y real yield vs peer real yield
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 2s10s. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through rates, discount-rate pressure, or funding conditions.

factors.name: 2Y yield
factors.derived: 2-year government bond yield trend and level
factors.effect: Primary effect: The 2Y yield is a direct policy-expectations input for 2s10s; higher 2Y yield means stronger front-end rate pressure.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: 10Y yield
factors.derived: 10-year government bond yield trend and level
factors.effect: Primary effect: The 10Y yield is the direct macro-rate input for 2s10s; higher readings mean more long-rate pressure.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Yield curve
factors.derived: Official Treasury curve spread 10Y minus 2Y: 0.370 percentage points on 2026-07-17; previous 0.410 percentage points on 2026-07-16
factors.effect: For the U.S. 2s10s Yield Curve, this curve spread is the direct input. Steepening usually points to changing growth or inflation pressure; flattening/inversion can warn of restrictive policy or growth stress.
factors.source: U.S. Treasury official Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates

factors.name: Real yields
factors.derived: 10Y TIPS real yield trend and level
factors.effect: Primary effect: Real yields affect 2s10s through inflation-adjusted return and discount-rate pressure.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Breakeven inflation
factors.derived: 5Y and 10Y breakeven inflation
factors.effect: Primary effect: Breakevens affect 2s10s through inflation expectations and real-yield pressure.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: PCE pressure
factors.derived: PCE and Core PCE inflation readings
factors.effect: Primary effect: Core PCE is important for policy expectations; hotter PCE usually supports rate/yield pressure and can support the dollar side of 2s10s.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: GDP / growth trend
factors.derived: GDP growth, nowcast, or growth surprise vs prior/forecast
factors.effect: Primary effect: Stronger growth usually raises yield pressure for 2s10s; weaker growth usually lowers yields and raises slowdown risk.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Retail sales / consumer demand
factors.derived: Retail sales headline/control group and surprise
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Consumer demand affects 2s10s through growth expectations and policy reaction.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Credit spreads
factors.derived: High-yield and investment-grade spreads vs history
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Credit spreads affect 2s10s through risk appetite and funding-stress conditions.
factors.source: FRED / Federal Reserve public-source candidate with series rights check
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Financial conditions
factors.derived: Financial conditions index or composite of rates, dollar, equities, and credit
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Financial conditions affect 2s10s through liquidity, credit availability, and risk appetite.
factors.source: FRED / Federal Reserve public-source candidate with series rights check
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Open interest
factors.derived: Futures open interest trend and change
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Open interest helps show conviction behind futures positioning for 2s10s. Rising OI with a supportive COT read adds weight; falling OI weakens confirmation.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Crude inventories
factors.derived: Weekly U.S. commercial crude oil stock change
factors.effect: Context only: Crude inventory draws usually support oil-linked assets by signaling tighter supply; builds usually pressure them. Use it to confirm or challenge the read, not as the main driver.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Cushing inventories
factors.derived: Cushing crude stock level and weekly change
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 2s10s. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: Product inventories
factors.derived: Gasoline and distillate stock changes
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 2s10s. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: Refinery utilization
factors.derived: Percent of refinery capacity utilized
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 2s10s. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: Product supplied / demand
factors.derived: EIA product supplied for gasoline, distillates, and total products
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 2s10s. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: U.S. production
factors.derived: U.S. crude or natural gas production estimate
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 2s10s. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: Imports / exports
factors.derived: Petroleum or commodity trade flow changes
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 2s10s. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: OPEC policy
factors.derived: OPEC official production targets, cuts, increases, compliance notes, and public monthly releases
factors.effect: Context only: OPEC cuts usually support oil by reducing supply; production increases or weak compliance usually pressure the oil balance. Use it to confirm or challenge the read, not as the main driver.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Natural gas storage
factors.derived: Weekly gas storage change and storage vs 5-year average
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 2s10s. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: Weather HDD/CDD
factors.derived: Heating-degree days, cooling-degree days, and public weather/forecast deviations where available
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 2s10s. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: LNG exports / power burn
factors.derived: LNG export flows and gas used for electricity generation
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 2s10s. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: WASDE ending stocks
factors.derived: USDA WASDE ending stocks for wheat, corn, soybeans, cotton
factors.effect: Low direct effect on 2s10s. Watch it only if it spills into food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Stock/use ratio
factors.derived: Ending stocks divided by total use
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 2s10s. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Crop progress / condition
factors.derived: Planting, emergence, harvest progress, and good/excellent crop ratings
factors.effect: Low direct effect on 2s10s. Watch it only if it spills into food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Export sales
factors.derived: Weekly export sales and shipments
factors.effect: Low direct effect on 2s10s. Watch it only if it spills into food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Weather / drought
factors.derived: NOAA/NWS weather, drought monitor, rainfall, heat/freeze risk, and crop-region conditions where available
factors.effect: Low direct effect on 2s10s. Watch it only if it spills into food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Global supply competitors
factors.derived: Brazil/Argentina/Black Sea/EU/Australia supply where relevant
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 2s10s. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Ethanol / biofuel linkage
factors.derived: EIA/USDA ethanol production, corn use for ethanol, and biofuel-demand data
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 2s10s. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: COT spec direction
factors.derived: CFTC COT net speculative positioning for the mapped futures contract; live values appear after Refresh CFTC COT Lane runs.
factors.effect: Spec direction shows trend participation. Net long speculative exposure can support the asset; net short exposure can pressure it, with inversion applied for Treasury yield assets and USD-base FX pairs.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports

factors.name: COT spec weekly change
factors.derived: Weekly change in speculative net exposure from the CFTC COT report.
factors.effect: Weekly change shows whether specs are adding to or reducing the directional positioning read. Adding exposure in the supportive direction strengthens the signal; reducing exposure weakens it.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports

factors.name: COT crowding risk
factors.derived: Recent percentile/range position of speculative net exposure where available.
factors.effect: Crowding risk separates participation from fragility. Stretched long positioning can create unwind risk; stretched short positioning can create short-covering risk.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports

factors.name: COT commercial / hedger extreme
factors.derived: Producer/merchant/processor/user positioning for commodity futures when available.
factors.effect: Commercial/hedger positioning is not an automatic signal. Extreme commercial long exposure can suggest value/support context; extreme commercial short exposure can warn that speculative long exposure may be crowded.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports

factors.name: COT spec-commercial conflict
factors.derived: Comparison of speculative positioning against commercial/hedger positioning where available.
factors.effect: Conflict means specs and commercials are meaningfully opposed. The trend participation read can still work, but reversal or unwind risk deserves more attention.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports

factors.name: BLS CPI pressure
factors.derived: CPI all items (CUSR0000SA0) latest 332.57 on 2026-06-01; year-over-year change 3.73%; 3-month annualized pace 2.78%.
factors.effect: Direct effect: Stronger inflation usually supports higher U.S. rate pressure for U.S. 2s10s Yield Curve because markets expect tighter policy or firmer nominal yields; softer inflation reduces that pressure.
factors.source: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics public API

scoreChangeLog.summary: 2s10s is broadly stable versus the previous saved snapshot.

assets.name: U.S. 5s30s Yield Curve
assets.mainConflict: BLS CPI energy pressure (bls, +1.40) vs Yield curve (treasury, -1.25)
assets.quick: Live-only scoring shows positive raw pressure from 26 counted rows. Top driver: BLS CPI energy pressure (bls, +1.40). Raw score is uncapped, source-weighted, and excludes non-live rows.

factors.name: Policy rate
factors.derived: Current central bank target rate or policy band
factors.effect: Primary effect: Higher policy-rate expectations usually push yield pressure higher for 5s30s; easier policy usually lowers rate pressure.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Rate differentials
factors.derived: Relevant yield spread between countries or curves, usually 2Y and 10Y comparisons
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 5s30s. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through rates, discount-rate pressure, or funding conditions.

factors.name: Real yield differentials
factors.derived: Inflation-adjusted yield spread, often 10Y real yield vs peer real yield
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 5s30s. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through rates, discount-rate pressure, or funding conditions.

factors.name: 2Y yield
factors.derived: 2-year government bond yield trend and level
factors.effect: Primary effect: The 2Y yield is a direct policy-expectations input for 5s30s; higher 2Y yield means stronger front-end rate pressure.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: 10Y yield
factors.derived: 10-year government bond yield trend and level
factors.effect: Primary effect: The 10Y yield is the direct macro-rate input for 5s30s; higher readings mean more long-rate pressure.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Yield curve
factors.derived: Official Treasury curve spread 30Y minus 5Y: 0.780 percentage points on 2026-07-17; previous 0.810 percentage points on 2026-07-16
factors.effect: For the U.S. 5s30s Yield Curve, this curve spread is the direct input. Steepening usually points to changing growth or inflation pressure; flattening/inversion can warn of restrictive policy or growth stress.
factors.source: U.S. Treasury official Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates

factors.name: Real yields
factors.derived: 10Y TIPS real yield trend and level
factors.effect: Primary effect: Real yields affect 5s30s through inflation-adjusted return and discount-rate pressure.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Breakeven inflation
factors.derived: 5Y and 10Y breakeven inflation
factors.effect: Primary effect: Breakevens affect 5s30s through inflation expectations and real-yield pressure.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: PCE pressure
factors.derived: PCE and Core PCE inflation readings
factors.effect: Primary effect: Core PCE is important for policy expectations; hotter PCE usually supports rate/yield pressure and can support the dollar side of 5s30s.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: GDP / growth trend
factors.derived: GDP growth, nowcast, or growth surprise vs prior/forecast
factors.effect: Primary effect: Stronger growth usually raises yield pressure for 5s30s; weaker growth usually lowers yields and raises slowdown risk.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Retail sales / consumer demand
factors.derived: Retail sales headline/control group and surprise
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Consumer demand affects 5s30s through growth expectations and policy reaction.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Credit spreads
factors.derived: High-yield and investment-grade spreads vs history
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Credit spreads affect 5s30s through risk appetite and funding-stress conditions.
factors.source: FRED / Federal Reserve public-source candidate with series rights check
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Financial conditions
factors.derived: Financial conditions index or composite of rates, dollar, equities, and credit
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Financial conditions affect 5s30s through liquidity, credit availability, and risk appetite.
factors.source: FRED / Federal Reserve public-source candidate with series rights check
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Open interest
factors.derived: Futures open interest trend and change
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Open interest helps show conviction behind futures positioning for 5s30s. Rising OI with a supportive COT read adds weight; falling OI weakens confirmation.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Crude inventories
factors.derived: Weekly U.S. commercial crude oil stock change
factors.effect: Context only: Crude inventory draws usually support oil-linked assets by signaling tighter supply; builds usually pressure them. Use it to confirm or challenge the read, not as the main driver.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Cushing inventories
factors.derived: Cushing crude stock level and weekly change
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 5s30s. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: Product inventories
factors.derived: Gasoline and distillate stock changes
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 5s30s. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: Refinery utilization
factors.derived: Percent of refinery capacity utilized
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 5s30s. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: Product supplied / demand
factors.derived: EIA product supplied for gasoline, distillates, and total products
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 5s30s. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: U.S. production
factors.derived: U.S. crude or natural gas production estimate
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 5s30s. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: Imports / exports
factors.derived: Petroleum or commodity trade flow changes
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 5s30s. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: OPEC policy
factors.derived: OPEC official production targets, cuts, increases, compliance notes, and public monthly releases
factors.effect: Context only: OPEC cuts usually support oil by reducing supply; production increases or weak compliance usually pressure the oil balance. Use it to confirm or challenge the read, not as the main driver.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Natural gas storage
factors.derived: Weekly gas storage change and storage vs 5-year average
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 5s30s. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: Weather HDD/CDD
factors.derived: Heating-degree days, cooling-degree days, and public weather/forecast deviations where available
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 5s30s. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: LNG exports / power burn
factors.derived: LNG export flows and gas used for electricity generation
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 5s30s. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: WASDE ending stocks
factors.derived: USDA WASDE ending stocks for wheat, corn, soybeans, cotton
factors.effect: Low direct effect on 5s30s. Watch it only if it spills into food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Stock/use ratio
factors.derived: Ending stocks divided by total use
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 5s30s. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Crop progress / condition
factors.derived: Planting, emergence, harvest progress, and good/excellent crop ratings
factors.effect: Low direct effect on 5s30s. Watch it only if it spills into food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Export sales
factors.derived: Weekly export sales and shipments
factors.effect: Low direct effect on 5s30s. Watch it only if it spills into food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Weather / drought
factors.derived: NOAA/NWS weather, drought monitor, rainfall, heat/freeze risk, and crop-region conditions where available
factors.effect: Low direct effect on 5s30s. Watch it only if it spills into food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Global supply competitors
factors.derived: Brazil/Argentina/Black Sea/EU/Australia supply where relevant
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 5s30s. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Ethanol / biofuel linkage
factors.derived: EIA/USDA ethanol production, corn use for ethanol, and biofuel-demand data
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on 5s30s. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: COT spec direction
factors.derived: CFTC COT net speculative positioning for the mapped futures contract; live values appear after Refresh CFTC COT Lane runs.
factors.effect: Spec direction shows trend participation. Net long speculative exposure can support the asset; net short exposure can pressure it, with inversion applied for Treasury yield assets and USD-base FX pairs.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports

factors.name: COT spec weekly change
factors.derived: Weekly change in speculative net exposure from the CFTC COT report.
factors.effect: Weekly change shows whether specs are adding to or reducing the directional positioning read. Adding exposure in the supportive direction strengthens the signal; reducing exposure weakens it.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports

factors.name: COT crowding risk
factors.derived: Recent percentile/range position of speculative net exposure where available.
factors.effect: Crowding risk separates participation from fragility. Stretched long positioning can create unwind risk; stretched short positioning can create short-covering risk.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports

factors.name: COT commercial / hedger extreme
factors.derived: Producer/merchant/processor/user positioning for commodity futures when available.
factors.effect: Commercial/hedger positioning is not an automatic signal. Extreme commercial long exposure can suggest value/support context; extreme commercial short exposure can warn that speculative long exposure may be crowded.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports

factors.name: COT spec-commercial conflict
factors.derived: Comparison of speculative positioning against commercial/hedger positioning where available.
factors.effect: Conflict means specs and commercials are meaningfully opposed. The trend participation read can still work, but reversal or unwind risk deserves more attention.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports

factors.name: BLS CPI pressure
factors.derived: CPI all items (CUSR0000SA0) latest 332.57 on 2026-06-01; year-over-year change 3.73%; 3-month annualized pace 2.78%.
factors.effect: Direct effect: Stronger inflation usually supports higher U.S. rate pressure for U.S. 5s30s Yield Curve because markets expect tighter policy or firmer nominal yields; softer inflation reduces that pressure.
factors.source: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics public API

scoreChangeLog.summary: 5s30s is broadly stable versus the previous saved snapshot.

assets.name: U.S. Real Yield Proxy
assets.mainConflict: BLS CPI energy pressure (bls, +2.00) vs BLS payroll growth (bls, -1.00)
assets.quick: Live-only scoring shows positive raw pressure from 28 counted rows. Top driver: BLS CPI energy pressure (bls, +2.00). Raw score is uncapped, source-weighted, and excludes non-live rows.

factors.name: Policy rate
factors.derived: Current central bank target rate or policy band
factors.effect: Primary effect: Policy rates affect RealYld mainly through currency, financing-cost, and demand pressure.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Rate differentials
factors.derived: Relevant yield spread between countries or curves, usually 2Y and 10Y comparisons
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on RealYld. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through rates, discount-rate pressure, or funding conditions.

factors.name: Real yield differentials
factors.derived: Inflation-adjusted yield spread, often 10Y real yield vs peer real yield
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on RealYld. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through rates, discount-rate pressure, or funding conditions.

factors.name: 2Y yield
factors.derived: Official Treasury 2 Yr par yield: 4.18% on 2026-07-17
factors.effect: Higher 2Y yields usually mean stronger front-end policy pressure; lower 2Y yields reduce that pressure. The effect is direct for rate assets and indirect for other assets through policy, dollar, and risk channels.
factors.source: U.S. Treasury official Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates

factors.name: 10Y yield
factors.derived: Official Treasury 10 Yr par yield: 4.55% on 2026-07-17
factors.effect: Higher 10Y yields usually increase long-rate pressure and can pressure risk assets and gold; lower 10Y yields usually ease that pressure.
factors.source: U.S. Treasury official Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates

factors.name: Yield curve
factors.derived: Official Treasury curve spreads on 2026-07-17: 10Y-2Y 0.370 percentage points; 10Y-3M 0.700 percentage points.
factors.effect: Primary effect: The yield curve affects RealYld through growth expectations, policy tightness, and recession-risk signaling.
factors.source: U.S. Treasury official Daily Treasury Par Yield Curve Rates

factors.name: Real yields
factors.derived: 10Y TIPS real yield trend and level
factors.effect: Primary effect: Real yields affect RealYld through inflation-adjusted return and discount-rate pressure.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Breakeven inflation
factors.derived: 5Y and 10Y breakeven inflation
factors.effect: Primary effect: Breakevens are a direct market-implied inflation input for RealYld; rising breakevens mean stronger inflation-expectation pressure.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: PCE pressure
factors.derived: PCE and Core PCE inflation readings
factors.effect: Primary effect: PCE affects RealYld through the Fed-policy and inflation-expectations channel.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: GDP / growth trend
factors.derived: GDP growth, nowcast, or growth surprise vs prior/forecast
factors.effect: Primary effect: Growth affects RealYld through demand, capital flows, and policy expectations.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Retail sales / consumer demand
factors.derived: Retail sales headline/control group and surprise
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Consumer demand affects RealYld through growth expectations and policy reaction.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Credit spreads
factors.derived: High-yield and investment-grade spreads vs history
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Credit spreads affect RealYld through risk appetite and funding-stress conditions.
factors.source: FRED / Federal Reserve public-source candidate with series rights check
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Financial conditions
factors.derived: Financial conditions index or composite of rates, dollar, equities, and credit
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Financial conditions affect RealYld through liquidity, credit availability, and risk appetite.
factors.source: FRED / Federal Reserve public-source candidate with series rights check
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Open interest
factors.derived: Futures open interest trend and change
factors.effect: Secondary effect: Open interest helps show conviction behind futures positioning for RealYld. Rising OI with a supportive COT read adds weight; falling OI weakens confirmation.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Crude inventories
factors.derived: Weekly U.S. commercial crude oil stock change
factors.effect: Context only: Crude inventory draws usually support oil-linked assets by signaling tighter supply; builds usually pressure them. Use it to confirm or challenge the read, not as the main driver.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Cushing inventories
factors.derived: Cushing crude stock level and weekly change
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on RealYld. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: Product inventories
factors.derived: Gasoline and distillate stock changes
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on RealYld. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: Refinery utilization
factors.derived: Percent of refinery capacity utilized
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on RealYld. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: U.S. production
factors.derived: U.S. crude or natural gas production estimate
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on RealYld. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: Imports / exports
factors.derived: Petroleum or commodity trade flow changes
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on RealYld. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: OPEC policy
factors.derived: OPEC official production targets, cuts, increases, compliance notes, and public monthly releases
factors.effect: Context only: OPEC cuts usually support oil by reducing supply; production increases or weak compliance usually pressure the oil balance. Use it to confirm or challenge the read, not as the main driver.
factors.scoreReason: not live freshness: prototype sample / public-source candidate

factors.name: Weather HDD/CDD
factors.derived: Heating-degree days, cooling-degree days, and public weather/forecast deviations where available
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on RealYld. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: LNG exports / power burn
factors.derived: LNG export flows and gas used for electricity generation
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on RealYld. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through energy inflation, growth expectations, or broad risk sentiment.

factors.name: WASDE ending stocks
factors.derived: USDA WASDE ending stocks for wheat, corn, soybeans, cotton
factors.effect: Low direct effect on RealYld. Watch it only if it spills into food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Stock/use ratio
factors.derived: Ending stocks divided by total use
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on RealYld. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Crop progress / condition
factors.derived: Planting, emergence, harvest progress, and good/excellent crop ratings
factors.effect: Low direct effect on RealYld. Watch it only if it spills into food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Export sales
factors.derived: Weekly export sales and shipments
factors.effect: Low direct effect on RealYld. Watch it only if it spills into food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Weather / drought
factors.derived: NOAA/NWS weather, drought monitor, rainfall, heat/freeze risk, and crop-region conditions where available
factors.effect: Low direct effect on RealYld. Watch it only if it spills into food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Global supply competitors
factors.derived: Brazil/Argentina/Black Sea/EU/Australia supply where relevant
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on RealYld. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: Ethanol / biofuel linkage
factors.derived: EIA/USDA ethanol production, corn use for ethanol, and biofuel-demand data
factors.effect: Normally no direct effect on RealYld. Do not score it for this asset unless it creates an unusual shock through food inflation, supply shocks, or policy/risk spillover.

factors.name: COT spec direction
factors.derived: CFTC COT net speculative positioning for the mapped futures contract; live values appear after Refresh CFTC COT Lane runs.
factors.effect: Spec direction shows trend participation. Net long speculative exposure can support the asset; net short exposure can pressure it, with inversion applied for Treasury yield assets and USD-base FX pairs.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports

factors.name: COT spec weekly change
factors.derived: Weekly change in speculative net exposure from the CFTC COT report.
factors.effect: Weekly change shows whether specs are adding to or reducing the directional positioning read. Adding exposure in the supportive direction strengthens the signal; reducing exposure weakens it.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports

factors.name: COT crowding risk
factors.derived: Recent percentile/range position of speculative net exposure where available.
factors.effect: Crowding risk separates participation from fragility. Stretched long positioning can create unwind risk; stretched short positioning can create short-covering risk.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports

factors.name: COT commercial / hedger extreme
factors.derived: Producer/merchant/processor/user positioning for commodity futures when available.
factors.effect: Commercial/hedger positioning is not an automatic signal. Extreme commercial long exposure can suggest value/support context; extreme commercial short exposure can warn that speculative long exposure may be crowded.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports

factors.name: COT spec-commercial conflict
factors.derived: Comparison of speculative positioning against commercial/hedger positioning where available.
factors.effect: Conflict means specs and commercials are meaningfully opposed. The trend participation read can still work, but reversal or unwind risk deserves more attention.
factors.source: CFTC Commitments of Traders public reports

factors.name: EIA petroleum inventory balance
factors.derived: U.S. commercial crude oil stocks excluding SPR (W_EPC0_SAX_NUS_MBBL) latest 499,740 thousand barrels on 2016-09-30; previous 502,716 thousand barrels on 2016-09-23. Weekly change: -2,976 thousand barrels. | U.S. motor gasoline stocks (W_EPM0_SAX_NUS_MBBL) latest missing thousand barrels on None; previous missing thousand barrels on None. | U.S. distillate fuel oil stocks (W_EPD0_SAX_NUS_MBBL) latest missing thousand barrels on None; previous missing thousand barrels on None.
factors.effect: Contextual effect: EIA energy tightness can affect U.S. Real Yield Proxy through inflation pressure, rate expectations, and risk appetite. It is not the main direct driver.
factors.source: U.S. Energy Information Administration open data/API

factors.name: BLS CPI pressure
factors.derived: CPI all items (CUSR0000SA0) latest 332.57 on 2026-06-01; year-over-year change 3.73%; 3-month annualized pace 2.78%.
factors.effect: Direct effect: Stronger inflation usually supports higher U.S. rate pressure for U.S. Real Yield Proxy because markets expect tighter policy or firmer nominal yields; softer inflation reduces that pressure.
factors.source: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics public API
